what_it_does: Defines a React Sidebar component and navigation-section configuration for a ConceptV design-system sidebar, rendering grouped nav buttons, icons, search action, badges, counts, collapse control, and a system-health footer.
observations: NAV_SECTIONS is a file-local metadata array grouping navigation items into Workspace, System, Foundations, Build with, and AI & settings.
The Search everything item is treated as an action via isAction and calls onOpenSearch instead of selecting a view.
Counts and status indicators are driven by keys into the counts prop, including countKey, newKey, healthScore, and gaps.
The component renders ConceptV branding, a sidebar close button, grouped headings, nav buttons, a spacer, and a system status block.
The component is exposed globally through window.Sidebar.
standouts: Navigation structure is data-driven rather than hard-coded directly in JSX.
The sidebar combines view navigation, command-palette launch, notification dots, item counts, and health status.
Icon names, labels, counters, and action behavior are centralized in NAV_SECTIONS.
conventions: Uses a global React object and assigns the component to window rather than using ES module imports/exports.
Uses className-based styling with dsa-* classes and inline styling for the keyboard shortcut badge.
Uses optional chaining for onCollapse and onOpenSearch, but calls onSelect directly for normal navigation items.
Aliases React.useEffect as useEffect_sb even though it is not used in this file.
concerns: counts is dereferenced throughout without a local default, so an undefined counts prop would throw.
onSelect is called directly for non-action items and would throw if absent.
The inline shortcut badge style is embedded in the render path rather than represented by a class.
notes: A file-local sidebar implementation that turns a static nav schema into grouped ConceptV design-system navigation UI.
questions: Is counts guaranteed to be provided by every caller?
Is onSelect required for all non-action nav items?
Is useEffect_sb leftover from an earlier version or intended for future sidebar behavior?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the left-navigation role, though the actual sidebar covers workspace, system, foundations, build, settings, search, and health status rather than only asset classes.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make the ConceptV design system navigable through grouped destinations while keeping search, status, counters, and sidebar collapse close at hand.
novelty: The navigation schema mixes regular destinations, a command-style search action, badge counts, new-state dots, and health coverage display in one sidebar file.
contribution: Contributes a structured sidebar navigation model and React rendering for grouped design-system sections, action entry, badges, counts, collapse control, and health summary.
summary_for_embedding: This is a React Sidebar component backed by NAV_SECTIONS metadata for rendering the ConceptV design-system left navigation with grouped items, icons, counters, search action, and health status.
intention_for_embedding: This was for presenting a compact navigational shell around a design-system workspace, including quick search, section counts, status indicators, and sidebar hiding.

symbols.does: Renders the ConceptV sidebar navigation from NAV_SECTIONS and the provided active, callback, and counts props.
symbols.description: Sidebar is a React function component that displays the ConceptV wordmark, a collapse button, grouped navigation buttons, optional shortcut/count/new indicators, and a system-health footer. It branches click behavior so the search item opens search while other items call onSelect with their id.
